private.name: ClearSpace
private.rationale: Same sector (Space & Aerospace), same stage (Series A), post-money $400M

data.narrative:
Lunar Outpost is a Series A-stage space and aerospace company that has raised $23.6M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of five public-market peers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and a private peer set of eight companies in the same sector at similar or adjacent stages, including ClearSpace, Hubble Network, Stoke Space, and Aalyria.

Two valuation methods are applicable to the target. The stage-progression approach applies a 2.0x multiplier typical of Series A-to-next-stage advancement to the $23.6M raised proxy, yielding $47.2M. The peer-median method references five same-sector comparables spanning Series A and Series B stages; the group median valuation is $400M, with a range of $274M to $1.3B. Public-market anchoring cannot be applied, as the target's revenue is not disclosed; the six public Space & Aerospace peers trade at a 45.3x median price-to-sales multiple, but lacks materiality without revenue data.

The triangulated analysis across two applicable methods supports a valuation range of $40M (low) to $460M (high), with a median of $224M. The substantial gap between stage-progression ($47M) and peer-median ($400M) reflects early-stage data scarcity and the wide dispersion in private peer valuations. Key uncertainties include the target's revenue traction, customer pipeline, and technological differentiation relative to higher-valued peers in the cohort.
